title: Our 800th Episode Ask-Us-Anything Mailbag. Plus: ‘Elio’ and ‘How to Train Your Dragon.’
format: The 800th episode is an all-listener mailbag — L.A. theaters, a film-class syllabus, movies watched with mortified parents, most-rewatched titles, one-star grudges — before reviews of Pixar's 'Elio' and the live-action 'How to Train Your Dragon.'

films.title: A Few Good Men
films.note: One of Amanda's current Letterboxd top four, which she hasn't touched in four or five years.

films.title: His Girl Friday
films.note: Also in the top four.

films.title: Marie Antoinette
films.note: The Sofia Coppola film, third of the four.

films.title: Four Weddings and a Funeral
films.note: The fourth — or possibly Working Girl; she wasn't sure.

films.title: Working Girl
films.note: Her profile icon is Melanie Griffith here, which is what threw her.

segments.heading: Is 2025 shaping up to be a great movie year?
segments.note: John's question — how do you know a good slate when you see one

films.title: F1: The Movie
films.note: Both name it the likeliest crowd-pleaser of the summer so far; full review promised for Friday.

films.title: 28 Years Later
films.note: A standout for Sean, a smart zombie movie that reached people.

films.title: Materialists
films.note: Not a perfect movie, but a durable discussion point that's in the culture.

films.title: Sinners
films.note: With 28 Years Later, one of the two films squarely in Sean's zone that also found an audience.

films.title: Final Destination Bloodlines
films.note: A surprise favorite — the MRI-machine clip was when Sean knew it had the juice.

films.title: Black Bag
films.note: Minor Soderbergh, but Amanda's guy.

films.title: The Phoenician Scheme
films.note: Received as minor Wes Anderson, which they'd expected.

films.title: Warfare
films.note: In the batch of movies they had a good feeling about going in.

films.title: The Shrouds
films.note: Also filed under 'at least interesting to me.'

films.title: Mickey 17
films.note: A letdown for many; Sean lands mixed.

films.title: Eddington
films.note: Both were seeing it that night — one of July's big question marks.

films.title: Superman
films.note: A hard-to-predict July tentpole for a studio (Warner Bros.) whose slate Sean quietly rates.

films.title: The Fantastic Four: First Steps
films.note: The other July Marvel unknown with a lot riding on it.

films.title: Jurassic World Rebirth
films.note: 'Plus dinosaurs' — the other July wild card.

films.title: One Battle After Another
films.note: The fall movie they're most confident will rock, box office aside.

films.title: Barbie
films.note: Half of the 2023 exception — the year everyone planned vacations around Barbenheimer.

films.title: Oppenheimer
films.note: The other half; Nolan as the single biggest director on the planet.

films.title: The Odyssey
films.note: Nolan's next — don't plan your vacation yet.

films.title: Past Lives
films.note: The rare 2023 standout that had actually come out by this point in the calendar.

films.title: The Zone of Interest
films.note: One of the 2023 titles that hadn't been released yet at midyear — a fall awards movie.

films.title: Killers of the Flower Moon
films.note: Same — proof that 2023's best arrived in the back half.

films.title: 28 Days Later
films.note: Made 26 years earlier; the smart-zombie forebear.

segments.heading: The best movie theaters in L.A.

films.title: Gone Girl
films.note: One of the Friend-of-the-Fest introductions Sean has done at the Aero.

films.title: The Talented Mr. Ripley
films.note: Another Aero introduction.

films.title: The Life of Chuck
films.note: Cited as a first-run title screening on film at the Vista.

films.title: The Brutalist
films.note: Sean saw it at the Vista, alone in an enormous room.

films.title: Nope
films.note: He's booked to see it in 70mm on his birthday at the Egyptian.

films.title: Die Hard with a Vengeance
films.note: Watched at Vidiots on Father's Day — an absolute ball.

segments.heading: The most uncomfortable movie you watched with a parent

films.title: Caddyshack
films.note: Campbell's example: his dad shot across the room to cover his eyes.

films.title: Uncut Gems
films.note: The worse-when-you're-older version of the same horror.

films.title: National Lampoon's Christmas Vacation
films.note: Amanda's Aunt Betty covered her eyes at the pool dream sequence.

films.title: National Lampoon's Vacation
films.note: The one she might actually be thinking of — the perfume-counter pool scene.

films.title: Jerry Maguire
films.note: Amanda's foundational memory: the Kelly Preston sex scene, seated next to her mother.

films.title: Sliver
films.note: Sean's topper — his dad kept the whole family watching the Joe Eszterhas thriller far longer than he should have.

films.title: Basic Instinct
films.note: Sharon Stone's prior film, invoked for context.

films.title: Center Stage
films.note: Sean fired it up for Knox; the PG-13 sex scene drew 'are they doing ballet at home?'

segments.heading: The movies that floored you this century
segments.note: Joshua's question — Sean leaned on debuts, no expectations going in

films.title: Memento
films.note: Knocked him out despite his up-and-down history with Nolan; premiered at Venice in 2000, eligible in 2001.

films.title: Ex Machina
films.note: Alex Garland's directorial debut and one of the first A24 movies, before that was a category.

films.title: Whiplash
films.note: A huge one he's returned to many times.

films.title: Raw
films.note: The French cannibal film he really, really loved.

films.title: In Bruges
films.note: Martin McDonagh's directorial debut.

films.title: Exit Through the Gift Shop
films.note: Banksy in his heyday; Sean liked how it was structured and made.

films.title: Borat
films.note: Hooting and hollering in the theater — 'is this really happening?'

films.title: Tár
films.note: Amanda's pick: shifting in her seat with excitement, then bursting out laughing at the ending.

films.title: Napoleon Dynamite
films.note: Seen in college at the Nugget; an instant quote machine.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: Seen together at the Chinese premiere; the room breathing as one at the end.

films.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
films.note: Seen in a New York blizzard — 'wow, what is this?'

films.title: La La Land
films.note: In the ArcLight Dome, on the largest screen possible; Amanda stands by the ending.

films.title: Babylon
films.note: The ending gave her thoughts, but the middle two hours were really good.

segments.heading: An intro-to-film syllabus, one per decade

films.title: The Exorcist
films.note: Horror, the '70s anchor.

films.title: Halloween (2007)
films.note: The Rob Zombie remake, offered to show a shift in transgression.

films.title: Urban Legend
films.note: Part of the post-Scream wave.

films.title: I Know What You Did Last Summer
films.note: Jennifer Love Hewitt as the real lead of the original.

films.title: I Know What You Did Last Summer (2025)
films.note: The new reboot with Madelyn Cline; a '90s-horror canon watch is planned with CR.

films.title: The Graduate
films.note: Coming-of-age starting point.

films.title: American Graffiti
films.note: Both hosts' first thought for the '70s.

films.title: Old Yeller
films.note: A rare pre-'60s film made about a child that adults would watch.

films.title: The Breakfast Club
films.note: The John Hughes '80s pick.

films.title: Now and Then
films.note: The girl version of the '90s coming-of-age movie.

films.title: The Sandlot
films.note: The sports version.

films.title: Clueless
films.note: A great shout from Amanda.

films.title: Fast Times at Ridgemont High
films.note: Could slot in the '80s too.

films.title: Superbad
films.note: The clear 2000s pick.

films.title: The Virgin Suicides
films.note: Filed to the 2000s, but really any Sofia — Lost in Translation, Marie Antoinette.

films.title: Lady Bird
films.note: Probably the best of the 2010s.

films.title: It Happened One Night
films.note: Romance starting point.

films.title: Sabrina
films.note: A '50s Billy Wilder option.

films.title: The Apartment
films.note: The Wilder pick they'd really want, if it counts for the '60s.

films.title: Doctor Zhivago
films.note: A '60s epic-romance option.

films.title: My Fair Lady
films.note: Offered despite Amanda's anti-Audrey-Hepburn stance.

films.title: The Way We Were
films.note: The '70s love story.

films.title: Moonstruck
films.note: One of the greats.

films.title: The Notebook
films.note: The 2000s signature, set in the '60s.

films.title: Fifty Shades of Grey
films.note: Amanda's 2010s romance pick.

films.title: Phantom Thread
films.note: Sean's counter.

films.title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
films.note: Where Madelyn Cline played the young girlfriend, which Amanda had memory-holed.

segments.heading: The movies you've seen the most
segments.note: Kevin's question — the great personality test

films.title: Clerks
films.note: Top of Sean's off-the-dome VHS-era list.

films.title: Citizen Kane
films.note: Watched over and over after turning 16 and getting into cinema.

films.title: The Wizard of Oz
films.note: First seen at 5, then on repeat as he got older.

films.title: Saltburn
films.note: Sean's modern dorm-room movie — get drunk, play cards, wait for Barry Keoghan.

films.title: You've Got Mail
films.note: One of Amanda's two Nora Ephrons at the top of her list.

films.title: Singin' in the Rain
films.note: True even before she introduced it to her son.

films.title: Father of the Bride
films.note: The Charles Shyer / Nancy Meyers version.

films.title: Apollo 13
films.note: She had the VHS.

films.title: Mamma Mia!
films.note: Taylor's premise — a Sean blind spot.

films.title: Medicine Man
films.note: First on Sean's Letterboxd watchlist despite being a McTiernan fan; a glitch in the matrix.

films.title: Basic
films.note: The other unseen McTiernan he's heard is absolutely crazy.

films.title: Poltergeist
films.note: Amanda has seen this, but not The Exorcist or Texas Chain Saw.

films.title: Victor/Victoria
films.note: A Julie Andrews head, but she's never seen the Blake Edwards musical.

films.title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
films.note: Seen, but never on a big screen — she bought 70mm Festival tickets to fix that.

segments.heading: Phones and talkers at the movies

films.title: Moneyball
films.note: At the Academy Museum, with Bennett Miller seated nearby, the people behind Sean narrated the whole film ('Brad Pitt is so sexy').

films.title: A Minecraft Movie
films.note: The 'Chicken Jockey' chaos — a movie made for 10-year-olds to be insane about.

films.title: La Strada
films.note: Sean's deadpan counter: 'you were in Minecraft, look at La Strada.'

segments.heading: Star ratings and the one-star hall of shame
segments.note: Tucker asks Sean; Andrew asks Amanda about Romy Mars

films.title: Free Guy
films.note: Probably a one-star for Sean — a deeply cynical, corrosive thing.

films.title: Dolittle
films.note: Half a star; one of the last pre-pandemic movies, and Amanda was furious.

films.title: Trolls
films.note: One star — the original, not World Tour.

films.title: The Prom
films.note: One star for the Ryan Murphy musical.

films.title: Man of Steel
films.note: A very angry review; the lone star is for Kevin Costner's performance.

films.title: Madame Web
films.note: A one-star Amanda insists is at least 1.5 — the Pepsi can, come on.

films.title: Fast X
films.note: One star. Put it on the podcast, not the newspaper.

films.title: Megalopolis
films.note: Where Sofia Coppola's daughter Romy Mars appeared; Amanda is pro her 'A-Lister' single.

segments.heading: The one popcorn movie to recommend
segments.note: Mike sees a movie or two a year — which yet-to-open 2025 title

films.title: Avatar: Fire and Ash
films.note: Sean's gut answer — the Avatar movies are an incredible visual feast in a theater.

films.title: Nosferatu (1922)
films.note: Public domain now and free on Tubi; Sean says access beats scarcity, even with a Tide ad in the middle. Amanda won't watch anything with commercials.

segments.heading: The directors who'd make you a star
segments.note: Aiden and Georgia — ascending, and an original kids' movie

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: Sean's example of a film that took Margot Robbie from A- to A+, even as a third lead.

films.title: Close Encounters of the Third Kind
films.note: Georgia's kids-movie question, answered through the arc of Spielberg's view of childhood.

films.title: E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial
films.note: The next step in that arc.

films.title: Mrs. Doubtfire
films.note: Sean would watch Denis Villeneuve's version, but it's not really his lane.

segments.heading: ‘Elio’
segments.note: Pixar's alien-abduction adventure — its worst opening ever

films.title: Elio
films.note: Directed by three (Domee Shi, Madeline Sharafian, Adrian Molina); the biggest bomb in Pixar history. Sean found it bottom-tier and boring; Amanda thought it fine and moving through a kid's eyes. A late America Ferrera mother role was rewritten into Zoe Saldana's aunt — you can feel the mid-production shift.

films.title: Turning Red
films.note: Domee Shi's prior film.

films.title: Coco
films.note: Adrian Molina co-wrote it; he left Elio mid-production to direct Coco 2, Pixar's last original mega-sensation.

films.title: Star Wars
films.note: 'E.T. and Star Wars exist' — Amanda's argument for just showing kids those instead; the creature design echoes the cantina, not quite as good.

films.title: Flight of the Navigator
films.note: Part of the post-E.T. boy-sci-fi wave Elio descends from.

films.title: The Black Hole
films.note: In Sean's lineage of Disney/Pixar 'failed boy movies' that never quite pull people in.

films.title: Inside Out 2
films.note: The massive outlier that complicates any 'Pixar crisis' theory.

films.title: Moana 2
films.note: One of the huge event-title outliers, alongside Lilo & Stitch and Inside Out 2.

films.title: Mufasa: The Lion King
films.note: 'Lion King 2' — a quiet ~$700M hit.

films.title: Luca
films.note: Another original that struggled in theaters.

films.title: Onward
films.note: A very short theatrical shelf life before COVID hit.

films.title: Encanto
films.note: Huge, but during COVID and driven by the virality of 'We Don't Talk About Bruno.'

films.title: Wish
films.note: One of four Disney non-sequels since Moana that made only $255M domestic combined.

films.title: The Super Mario Bros. Movie
films.note: Cited with Barbie as the unexploited-IP benchmark audiences now want.

films.title: Sinners
films.note: 'How do we get Sinners for kids movies?' — back a generational talent and a great idea, don't chase a formula.

films.title: Hoppers
films.note: The next Pixar original; its lizard-emoji stinger reportedly played after Elio.

films.title: Incredibles 3
films.note: Further out on the slate.

films.title: Coco 2
films.note: The sequel Molina left Elio to make.

segments.heading: ‘How to Train Your Dragon’
segments.note: Universal's live-action remake — and the case against it

films.title: How to Train Your Dragon
films.note: Dean DeBlois's live-action remake: nearly the same script, 35 minutes longer, strong effects, Gerard Butler the best part. Sean calls it creatively empty but understands the money — already ~$365M. Nico Parker's Astrid gets a slightly stronger role.

films.title: How to Train Your Dragon (2010)
films.note: The animated original, also co-directed by DeBlois; Amanda watched it with her daughter, who wanted more of Astrid.

films.title: The Northman
films.note: The remake's night-time Viking pillaging felt too scary for kids in the way The Northman did.

films.title: Robin Hood
films.note: The animated Disney film Knox loves — 'my friend Sir Hiss.'

films.title: Gabby's Dollhouse: The Movie
films.note: The other kids' option Sean will take Knox to if he has to.

films.title: Lawrence of Arabia
films.note: Just added to the 70mm Festival — Sean tried to buy tickets mid-podcast.

films.title: Eddington
films.note: Floated as a 'kids movie' bit.

films.title: Wicked: For Good
films.note: One of the two big winter kids' events.

films.title: Zootopia 2
films.note: The other.

films.title: Zootopia
films.note: Celine Song's desert-island pick over Children of Men; Sean still hasn't seen it.

films.title: Children of Men
films.note: Celine Song's stated favorite, set aside for the desert-island framing.

Borat.title: Borat: Cultural Learnings of America for Make Benefit Glorious Nation of Kazakhstan

I Know What You Did Last Summer (2025).title: I Know What You Did Last Summer
